_note: hand-curated bias and credibility ratings for philippine news outlets. based on cmfr assessments and documented editorial patterns.

_credibility_scale.highly_reliable: 0.80–1.0 — strong editorial standards, IFCN-certified or equivalent
_credibility_scale.needs_context: 0.40–0.59 — accurate but strong slant or incomplete coverage
_credibility_scale.unreliable: 0.0–0.29 — documented history of false or misleading content

Rappler.credibility_explanation: IFCN-certified fact-checker with international press freedom awards. Strong editorial standards. Known left-leaning editorial stance.

GMA News.credibility_explanation: Major broadcast network with established editorial standards. Generally centrist but commercial pressures occasionally affect coverage depth.

Inquirer.credibility_explanation: One of the largest Philippine broadsheets. Strong investigative tradition but occasionally criticized for sensationalist headlines.

Philippine News Agency.credibility_explanation: Official government news agency. Factually accurate on public announcements but lacks editorial independence — coverage favors government narrative.

Business Mirror.credibility_explanation: Business and economic focus with generally solid reporting. Limited coverage of political and social issues.

Eagle News.credibility_explanation: Affiliated with Iglesia ni Cristo. Factually accurate on reported events but strong institutional bias affects story selection and framing.

Google News PH.credibility_explanation: Aggregator — credibility depends on sourced outlets. Useful for coverage breadth but not a primary source.

ABS-CBN News.credibility_explanation: Major broadcast network with strong investigative reporting history. 2020 broadcast shutdown has not significantly affected digital credibility.

PhilStar.credibility_explanation: Established broadsheet with solid news standards. Occasionally criticized for proximity to business interests.

Manila Bulletin.credibility_explanation: Long-established conservative outlet. Generally factual but editorial stance strongly favors establishment and business interests.

Manila Standard.credibility_explanation: Conservative editorial line with documented pro-establishment bias. Factual accuracy varies — verify claims independently.

One News PH.credibility_explanation: BeMediaGroup outlet with generally solid reporting standards. Relatively newer with less extensive editorial track record than legacy outlets.

CNN Philippines.credibility_explanation: International brand standards applied to local coverage. Strong on breaking news, occasional depth issues on complex local stories.

VERA Files.credibility_explanation: IFCN-certified Philippine fact-checker with rigorous methodology. One of the most credible sources in the registry.
